completed_tasks.name: Stabilize industry Google News discovery and publisher resolution
Refresh industry live regression coverage and source-split contracts
Re-establish honest fresh-process smoke and benchmark baselines
completed_tasks.progress: Artifact-backed smoke/generalization baselines now exist and are being used instead of same-process estimates.

completed_tasks.name: Reduce academic tail latency in live hidden-style runs
completed_tasks.progress: Retained via Semantic Scholar/OpenAlex overlap and academic upstream-query dedupe; isolated academic slice reached 9/10 grounded_success.

completed_tasks.name: Mixed grounded uplift without heavy mixed first-hop routing
completed_tasks.progress: Retained partial mixed local fast-path landed, but heavier mixed first-hop experiments (mixed-v2/v3) failed and were reverted.

blockers.description: Mixed hidden-like failures are still dominated by policy/industry source timeouts before dual-route evidence exists.
blockers.workaround: Keep primary route stable; avoid heavier mixed first-hop fan-out.

blockers.description: The mixed-first-hop structural experiment stayed 0/10 and regressed cases that previously at least returned insufficient_evidence.
blockers.workaround: Do not reintroduce first-hop mixed inflation without new artifact-backed evidence.

blockers.description: gen2-mixed-04, gen2-mixed-05, and gen2-mixed-10 repeatedly return one policy hit with no supplemental evidence path.
blockers.workaround: Investigate a cheap post-primary supplemental probe rather than first-hop mixed routing.

human_actions_pending.action: Load .env before any live /answer benchmark rerun
human_actions_pending.context: The local shell needs MINIMAX_KEY for fresh-process live benchmark runs.

decisions.decision: Keep normalize/tokenize utilities as generic infrastructure but stop expanding business-term marker lists for intent routing.
decisions.rationale: Normalization and tokenization are useful features; business vocabulary enumeration is not maintainable and did not generalize.

decisions.decision: Do not pursue a trainable four-class first-hop router right now.
decisions.rationale: The useful abstraction is stable primary-route evidence, not a heavier taxonomy that promotes more queries into mixed first-hop retrieval.

decisions.decision: Do not pursue heavier first-hop mixed routing right now.
decisions.rationale: Fresh-process mixed-v2/v3 stayed 0/10 and increased retrieval_failure without improving grounded success.

decisions.decision: Retain only the structural changes that already survived validation.
decisions.rationale: Semantic Scholar/OpenAlex overlap, academic upstream-query dedupe, and partial mixed local fast-path have artifact-backed or regression-backed value.

next_action: Inspect gen2-mixed-04, gen2-mixed-05, and gen2-mixed-10 from v47/v48 and design a cheap post-primary supplemental probe that only runs after primary policy evidence succeeds and budget remains.
context_notes: The biggest insight from this session is that the competition has probably been over-modeled as a four-class routing problem. Mixed is not paying its way as a heavier first-hop route. Keep primary route stable, preserve partial facts, and move complexity later in the pipeline where evidence and budget can justify it.
